sentence_reviews.text: Distillation transfers behavior from a teacher to a smaller student, while pruning removes low-value weights, channels, heads, experts, or activations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hinton arXiv abstract lines 42-44 describes compressing knowledge from ensembles into a single easier-to-deploy model; Li arXiv abstract lines 38-41 describes pruning/compressing weights and filters identified as having small effects on output accuracy.
sentence_reviews.notes: Teacher/student distillation and weight/filter pruning are supported; heads, experts, and activations are broader than the inspected sources.

sentence_reviews.text: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Supervised learning, Calibration, uncertainty & risk objectives.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Knowledge distillation, pruning & sparsification is modeled as depending on Supervised learning, Calibration, uncertainty & risk objectives and feeding downstream nodes such as Quantized inference & compressed deployment, Edge, on-device & neuromorphic deployment.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
